steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Sentence 1 reports that caffeic acid benefits the termite mushroom by combating free radicals. Sentence 2 reports that the acid also promotes cellular health in humans. The relationship is additive — a second benefit is being layered onto the first — so the transition must signal addition, not contrast or correction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Conversely,’: signals opposition or reversal; both sentences describe benefits of the same acid, so there is no contrast to introduce. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Moreover,’: signals an additional related point; expanding from the mushroom’s benefit to humans’ benefit is exactly an additive relationship. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Rather,’: signals correction or substitution of the prior idea; nothing in sentence 1 is being corrected or replaced. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘For example,’: signals that what follows is a specific instance of a general claim made before it; humans are not an example of mushrooms, so this does not fit the logical relationship. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
